mutations.change: Preserve transition insertion order rather than applying the reviewed stable database event ordering.
mutations.reason: The shuffled same-tic fixture has a distinct exact tuple order and dense audio ordinals.

mutations.change: Continue audio ordinals across logical tics instead of resetting each tic to zero.
mutations.reason: The multi-tic timeline explicitly checks the first ordinal for every represented tic.

mutations.change: Infer an automap sound for a control transition lacking an audio definition.
mutations.reason: The fixture contains a deliberately unmapped control transition that must remain silent.

mutations.change: Use intermission music D_INTER at map start rather than the reviewed E1M1 asset D_E1M1.
mutations.reason: The exact tic-zero music tuple and asset identity are independently frozen.

mutations.change: Emit E1M1 music on every response rather than only its authoritative map-start transition.
mutations.reason: A later response timeline explicitly contains no repeated music transition or tuple.

mutations.change: Replace database volume with a locally inferred distance-based browser value.
mutations.reason: The expected tuples and scheduled normalized gain pin the database-provided value.

mutations.change: Replace database separation with a locally inferred listener-angle browser value.
mutations.reason: The asymmetric fixture pins exact separation and its presentation-only pan conversion.

mutations.change: Silently discard a duplicate tuple and continue rather than failing closed.
mutations.reason: The accepted cursor test requires a duplicate error before any second scheduling.

mutations.change: Sort a reordered response locally and play it instead of rejecting non-increasing input.
mutations.reason: A lower tic after an accepted event must fail even if sorting would produce a timeline.

mutations.change: Coerce string numbers and clamp invalid tuple fields rather than rejecting malformed data.
mutations.reason: Focused invalid fixtures require strict types and bounds before cache or playback.

mutations.change: Fetch a sound from a static /assets path instead of POSTing DOOM_API.GET_ASSET.
mutations.reason: The browser request ledger allows only the exact AutoREST asset operation.

mutations.change: Query DOOM_ASSET_BLOB through an AutoREST table endpoint from the browser.
mutations.reason: Only DOOM_API and PUBLIC_HEALTH may be exposed; browser base-table requests fail.

mutations.change: Fetch and decode every occurrence even when the asset name is already in flight or decoded.
mutations.reason: Concurrent and sequential same-name occurrences must share one promise and buffer.

mutations.change: Key the decoded cache by event ordinal instead of canonical asset name.
mutations.reason: Two ordinals referencing DSPISTOL must produce one request and one decode.

mutations.change: Embed generated oscillator or copied PCM/music bytes in client source as a fallback.
mutations.reason: All playable bytes must originate in the seeded database asset returned by GET_ASSET.

mutations.change: Start queued AudioBuffer sources while AudioContext is suspended before user interaction.
mutations.reason: The browser fixture observes zero starts before click and exact starts afterward.

mutations.change: Discard events received before the user gesture rather than queueing them.
mutations.reason: The pre-gesture event must schedule once immediately after context resume.

mutations.change: Schedule all previously queued sources again on a repeated enable or explicit flush call.
mutations.reason: Pinned Chromium checks the exact one-time schedule count and complete database tuple order.

mutations.change: Use AudioContext currentTime or decode completion order to advance or suppress logical events.
mutations.reason: Gameplay tuple acceptance is cursor-based and independent of presentation timing.

mutations.change: Change fire or movement command fields when audio is disabled or decoding fails.
mutations.reason: A frozen command object remains byte-identical across every audio outcome.

mutations.change: Commit audio rows independently so an owning tic failure leaves playback events behind.
mutations.reason: The rollback fixture requires audio rows and all other tic effects to disappear together.

mutations.change: Read transitions without the session-token predicate and emit another session's sound.
mutations.reason: Two colliding tic/ordinal fixtures must remain isolated by the composite session key.

mutations.change: Hard-code CASE event_type mappings in the package instead of joining reviewed definitions.
mutations.reason: Source policy and a definition-driven novel fixture reject embedded dispatch.

mutations.change: Catch a decode or scheduling failure but still emit console.error in the successful browser flow.
mutations.reason: Strict page guards promote every console error, page error, or request failure to failure.
